title: Error exporting volume (podman volume export)
body:
### Issue Description

Cannot export volume to tarball using `podman volume export` command

### Steps to reproduce the issue

#### Command
```powershell
podman volume export db2ce --output db2ce.tar
```

### Describe the results you received

#### Output
```
Error: unmarshalling error into &errorhandling.ErrorModel{Because:"", Message:"", ResponseCode:0}, data "Not Found\n": invalid character 'N' looking for beginning of value
```

### Describe the results you expected

Volume exports to tarball, ofc.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Windows 11 25H2, Podman w/ WSL2

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Yes, the volume is there. Checked with `podman volume list` and `podman volume inspect <volume name>`.

> This appears to be another unmarshaling error.
> 
> @SalaryTheft Can you please check your volume names, etc.? It seems the volume that you want to export doesn't exist.
> 
> cc @mheon

> Version: 5.5.2

The server version is to old, you need to recreate the machine to pick up the latest image or try to update inside with `dnf update`.

The remote endpoints where only added in v5.6 https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/tag/v5.6.0

> This appears to be another unmarshaling error.

That is the one you fixed already in https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/0a9d5ca75da562c15d7d55eb2705af8be91b612d pretty sure
